rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF ROWENA VELONZA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #11, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/23/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) An accounting cannot be approved until the Court Investigation Unit report has been filed. NB: There is no need to file every bank statement for an accounting period for interim accountings. Only the statement(s) preceding the date of appointment and verifying the ending balance of an accounting are required for the FIRST accounting; for interim and final accountings, only the statement(s) verifying the ending balance is/are required to be filed unless otherwise requested by the Court. [Probate Code Section 2620(c)(2) and LRSF 14.34 D.] Please provide a courtesy copy of proof of service of the complete notice of hearing to the assigned examiner by 9/9/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ADRIENNE MARIE MORELLO
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/23/2025. The following issues must be addressed two weeks prior to that date: 1) Notice of Hearing (Form DE-120) is required for this proceeding and Box 1 must be checked (Probate Code Section 12201). 2) All notices must include language about remote appearances (San Francisco Local Rule 14.1.C.6). 3) The report of status must set forth the status of the estate including, but not limited to, allegations regarding notice pursuant to Probate Code Section 9202, disclosure of all creditor claims and their dispositions, requests for special notice, and any assignments or powers of attorney that may affect distribution (San Francisco Local Rule 14.35.J.1). 4) No proposed order has been received or lodged with the court (San Francisco Local Rule 14.7.A).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MAURICE ELLIS GOLDMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/14/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice mailed on 7/23/25 for a hearing on 8/5/25 was insufficient notice. (3) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (4) The report does not include all the disclosures required by LRSF 14.35.J. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 9/30/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAM H. WELLMAN 2004 TRUST, DATED APRIL 27, 2004 AS AMENDED AND RESTATED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For: (1) Court Order Finding That The 2004 Trust Was Amended By The 2017 Agreement To Remove Sunny V.H. Luong And His Family Members As Beneficiaries; And (2) Court Order Finding That Sunny V.H. Luang Is Not A Beneficiary Or Trustee Of The 2004 Trust Due To The Termination Of His Domestic Partnership With Decedent
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANDREW SHUEN CHARK LAM
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/3/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) The statutory fee is miscalculated. It must be amended, and notice of the amendment must be mailed at least 15 days before the 9/3/25 hearing (if not waived). (3) The initial Petition for Letters of Administration did not identify the decedent's parents and their dates of death, as required by LRSF 14.18 C.3 and D.1. Please do so now in the amendment to the petition. (4) The Petition for Letters of Administration also identified the petitioner and Arthur Lam as the successor trustees of the decedent's trust dated 12/21/19 but alleged that Decedent died intestate. Did he not have a pour-over will to his trust? Please provide a revised proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 8/20/25 in order to avoid a further continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Please make a note of it. Notice is not required to be given to nephews in an intestate estate, when their parents survived.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM A. GIBSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/17/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) A conservator has not yet been appointed for the incapacitated heir. If the (proposed) conservator is the petitioner, there will be a conflict of interest, and a guardian ad litem should be appointed for the heir/conservatee. (2) Petitioner's attorney reported at the hearing on 6/3/25 that John is in a care facility, but notice has only been sent to him at the same address as Petitioner. (3) The petitioner (who has not yet been appointed as the executor of the decedent's will) claims that it had always been the intent of the co-owners that she ultimately hold 100% ownership of "the property" but does not explain how or why this was everyone's intent or provide any written agreement to that effect. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 9/3/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JEAN D DOMINEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will dated 7/24/12. [Probate Code Section 8225.] No appearance is required. Status hearing will be set for 2/9/27. If a petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration is filed at least two weeks before that status hearing date, no appearance will be required on that date. (A supplement should stand on its own and merely provide additional information, not "respond" to issues.)
